channel.title: Folland's Real Analysis on mel-land
channel.description: Recent content in Folland's Real Analysis on mel-land

item.title: Problem Set 1
item.description: (Folland 5.1) If $ \mathcal{X} $ is a normed vector space over $ K $ ($ = \mathbb{R} $ or $ \mathbb{C} $), then addition and scalar multiplication are continuous from $ \mathcal{X}\times \mathcal{X} $ and $ K\times \mathcal{X} $ to $ \mathcal{X} $. Moreover, the norm is continuous from $ \mathcal{X} $ to $ [0,\infty) $; in fact, $ \left | ||x|| - ||y|| \right | \leq ||x-y|| $.
